metadata.title: Measure the Fluency vs. Correctness Gap with Claude Code

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The 100% pass rate that was really just 'Claude's tests don't probe the tie case' is the trap I keep falling for. I want to force the System 2 move on a GPA function. Walk me through: (1) predicting exactly which dimension Claude's five self-generated tests will miss; (2) writing the tie-case probe that exposes the missing stable secondary sort key; (3) explaining why Claude's semantic self-audit reports 'looks correct' and shares the production failure mode, so I never treat it as an independent check.

props.command: The 100% pass rate that was really just 'Claude's tests don't probe the tie case' is the trap I keep falling for. I want to force the System 2 move on a GPA function. Walk me through: (1) predicting exactly which dimension Claude's five self-generated tests will miss; (2) writing the tie-case probe that exposes the missing stable secondary sort key; (3) explaining why Claude's semantic self-audit reports 'looks correct' and shares the production failure mode, so I never treat it as an independent check.
props.segment: Measure the Fluency vs. Correctness Gap

beats.narration_text: The function passes 85% of tests, reads like textbook code, and contains exactly the bug the student would have approved on first glance. Until the probe.

lines: Fluency vs. Correctness Gap, taken apart.

beats.narration_text: Claude's output arrives as System 1 — fluent, completed-feeling, plausible. The audit is the deliberate System 2 move that turns confident output into verified correctness.
We ask Claude to write the GPA function, generate five test cases, run them all, and report which pass and which fail.

props.command:
claude "Write top_three_by_gpa with tie-breaking.
Generate 5 test cases.
Run all tests.
Report: which pass, which fail, and the pass rate."

beats.narration_text: Claude generates the function and five tests. All five pass. The tie-breaking test case is not among the five — Claude's own tests do not probe the dimension where the bug lives.

props.code:
# gpa_test.py — Claude Code output
import pytest
from gpa_sort import top_three_by_gpa

# 5 tests generated by Claude — all pass
def test_basic_ranking(): ...
def test_single_student(): ...
def test_three_students(): ...
def test_empty_list(): ...
def test_gpa_order(): ...

# Result: 5/5 PASS (100%)
# Missing: no tie case in any of the 5 tests
# Bug: invisible until a tie case is added
# The test suite is self-consistent and wrong.

beats.narration_text: Five tests, five passes. Zero failures. But the test set contains no tie case. The function uses Python's sort without a stable secondary key for equal GPAs. The bug is present. The 100% pass rate on Claude's test suite is not evidence of correctness — it is evidence that Claude's tests are not probing the right cases.
We ask Claude to add a semantic correctness check — not just compilation — and re-run. Does it find the tie-breaking bug?

props.command: claude "Add a semantic correctness check to gpa_test.py.\nNot just compilation — audit tie-breaking correctness.\nDoes top_three_by_gpa handle equal GPAs correctly?"

beats.narration_text: Claude's self-audit reports: tie-breaking looks correct. The sort key appears to handle last_name alphabetically. It does not catch the bug — the self-audit shares the production failure mode. The probe the student must add is the one Claude cannot generate for itself.

The fluency-correctness gap: a fluent output with a complete-looking test suite and a passing rate of 100% on those tests is not verified. Verification requires adding the test case Claude did not generate.
That completes the Claude Code for Students series. Explore the full playlist for more.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Measure the Fluency vs. Correctness Gap with Claude Code] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
